tables.title: Ruler
tables.description: Who holds power in the town, and how does their leadership shape its atmosphere and politics? Eberron’s rulers range from dragonmarked houses to ancient beings, each influenced by magic, industry, and shifting alliances.

entries.description: Does this noble owe allegiance to a crown of the Five Nations, a dragonmarked house, or both? Are they a figurehead for another power such as the Aurum or a patron in the Chamber? How do they enforce control in an age of airships and magewrights; do they rely on house contracts, Galifar-era law, or fear backed by hired Deneith swords?

Is the council made up of guild leaders, dragonmarked representatives, or wealthy merchants? How do they balance local needs with house charters and the interests of bodies like the Twelve or Morgrave University? Can outsiders such as adventurers gain a voice, or does the council keep decisions behind closed doors guarded by Medani advisers?

Who shapes the vote; a Brelish-style borough charter, a Zil-inspired civic club, or a house-backed slate? Do artificers, labourers, and merchants form blocs, and which promises bind the leader to Orien, Cannith, or local syndicates? Is the election clean, or do Sivis messages and Phiarlan rumours sway opinion?

What tradition defines the leadership: graduates of Arcanix, a circle of magewrights, or a priest-arcanist order? How does this power shape daily life; street lighting by continual flame, bound elementals in civic works, or licences for spellcasting? Do common folk trust the casters, or resent a caste that writes the rules in glyph and sigil?

Which dragonmarked house or Aurum consortium holds sway? Is the town centred on elemental binding (Cannith), transportation (Orien or Lyrandar), prospecting (Tharashk), or healing (Jorasco)? Is life run on company scrip, or do Kundarak-secured markets allow open trade? What rivalries simmer with other houses or local guilds?

What faith rules: a Thrane-aligned Silver Flame ministry, Seekers of the Blood of Vol, or a druidic sect such as the Children of Winter? Do clergy claim divine mandate, lean on relics, or draw strength from a manifest zone? Are the townsfolk devout, or do they obey a strict moral code to keep inquisitors away?

Why do soldiers govern? Is this a border fort near the Mournland, a reclaimed war zone, or a port seized after unrest? Do they serve a nation, House Deneith, or a victorious warlord? Are civilians grateful for protection or chafing under curfews, papers, and rations?

How does the commune maintain equality and order? Do druids and artificers distribute food and water with magic, or do house contracts quietly steer decisions? Is this a post-war experiment in the Eldeen style, a warforged settlement pursuing the Becoming God, or a fragile truce among survivors with shared chores and rotating stewards?

Why is there no formal leadership; disaster, rebellion, or deliberate choice? Is this a haven for smugglers and outcasts under the eye of Boromar fixers or Daask enforcers? Do informal powers such as gangs, houses, or secret societies direct outcomes while claiming there are no rules?

What ancient or immortal entity dominates the town: a Chamber-minded dragon, a slumbering daelkyr’s influence, or a buried warforged colossus? Do they rule openly or through agents and omens? Are the people fearful, reverent, or pragmatic partners to a power that cannot be ignored?

tables.title: Beliefs
tables.description: Beliefs in Eberron—whether religious, cultural, or philosophical—shape a town’s identity. The Last War shook old certainties; some cling to traditions while others pursue new paths.

entries.description: Does the town honour the Sovereign Host broadly, or elevate a patron such as Boldrei, Dol Arrah, or Onatar? Do some make quiet offerings to the Dark Six when storms rise and deals sour? Which customs bind the community, and which taboos divide it?

entries.value: Singular Faith (Silver Flame or Blood of Vol)
entries.description: Is this a bastion of the Silver Flame, sworn to protect the innocent and oppose fiends, or a Seeker community of the Blood of Vol that believes divinity lies within and death must be defied? Are they missionaries, reformers, or zealots? Do relics, miracles, or a manifest zone affirm their cause; how do they treat dissent?

Do Gatekeepers ward against aberrant incursions and planar rot, or do Ashbound radicals resist industrial magic? How do fields, rivers, and forests figure into rites; are elementals bargained with, or bound only under strict oaths?

Is reverence directed toward an ancient dragon, a hidden daelkyr, or a bound overlord of the Lords of Dust? Are townsfolk empowered by gifts and signs, or nudged by whispers and fear? Do outsiders brand it heresy, or a necessary pact that keeps worse fates at bay?

Is the Becoming God being assembled from sacred parts, or does a Lord of Blades sympathiser preach warforged supremacy? Are Cannith relics curated like scripture, and do people view artifice as a holy craft that will carry them into a new age?

Do scholars track the Draconic Prophecy in weathered stones and moon signs, or do meditative circles practise the Path of Light while spies of the Dreaming Dark sow guidance through shared dreams? Which faction’s reading of fate guides town policy?

entries.value: Civic Destiny (Aftermath of the Last War)
entries.description: Are they Cyran refugees set on rebuilding, a border town seeking recognition, or settlers with a mandate to restore a blasted vale? What founding myth directs investment and risk; how far will they go to make it real?

Why keep outsiders at bay; Aereni caution, trauma among Cyran survivors, or subtle Riedran pressure? Is the town polite but closed, or actively hostile? How do they treat adventurers who bring news, coin, and trouble in equal measure?
Is peace a creed rooted in Boldrei’s hearth or Arawai’s bounty? How do they respond to raiders and extortion; walls, tribute, or moral witness? What crisis could test their convictions?

Do local customs arise from a Thelanis-touched wood, a Mabar-tinged cemetery, or carved draconic runes in the hills? Are such beliefs dismissed by travellers, or taken seriously because the signs sometimes come true?

tables.title: Inside Dangers
tables.description: Internal dangers in Eberron often tie to houses, syndicates, and wartime scars. Threats grow from corrupt officials, arcane secrets, and ambitions that outstrip conscience.

entries.value: Rival Families (Dragonmarked Houses or Local Dynasties)
entries.description: Are Cannith subcontractors at odds with a Jorasco clinic, or do old nobles and merchant dynasties feud over a dragonshard claim? How public does the violence become; which heirs seek peace while others court the Aurum for muscle?

Are these rag-tag crews, Boromar smugglers, Daask bruisers from Droaam, or discreet operatives for a house? Do colours, house pins, or coded phrases mark territory? What contraband moves: dreamlily, dragonshards, illegal warforged parts, or cursed artefacts?

entries.value: Corrupt Constabulary (Bought by Houses or Crime Lords)
entries.description: Who owns the watch; a petty tyrant, a Boromar bookkeeper, or a house retainer with Medani warding contracts? How deep does the rot go; do troublemakers vanish, or get framed with Sivis-sealed evidence?
Is there a local Aurum enclave, a Boromar pipeline to Sharn, or a Daask warehouse for monstrous muscle? How far does influence reach; city hall, a Kundarak vault clerk, or a Cannith foreman?

Is a Thuranni or Phiarlan killer settling a contract, or is a warforged avenger hunting former abusers from the Last War? Are the killings political, house-targeted, or the work of a true predator? Do the characters end up on the list?

Who is kidnapping townsfolk; Karrnathi loyalists recruiting in secret, or a rogue Cannith binder feeding an illegal workshop? Who gets taken and for what tasks; can anyone escape and speak before they are silenced?

How harsh are conditions; twelve-hour shifts at a binding foundry or unsafe mines with poor wards? Do Cannith, Tharashk, or Sivis managers hide dangerous side effects? Are strikes broken by Deneith contractors, and do workers have allies beyond the town?

Who is targeted; changelings, aberrant-marked, or a zealot-defined heresy? Who stirs the mob; a demagogue, a frightened priest, or a rival house? Do the players expose lies and calm tempers, or does blood spill before truth prevails?

Do magebred hounds from a failed Vadalis trial prowl, or does something from Khyber slip through? Are ‘raids’ actually retaliation for a land grab against a Darguul band, or the work of lycanthropes hiding among the folk? What mistake or secret started it?

entries.value: Dark Cults (Dragon Below or Otherworldly Powers)
entries.description: Is a hidden sect serving the Dragon Below, an overlord’s agent, or a daelkyr scientist’s dream? What rite nears completion; what office do they already control? Will the town face madness, possession, or a planar breach if no one intervenes?

tables.title: Outside Dangers
tables.description: Wilderness and wider politics press on every settlement. From the Last War’s leftovers to planar intrusions, external threats force hard choices and swift action.

entries.description: Are these demobbed soldiers who never found peace, or hired blades serving a noble or house? Do they rob caravans, raid lightning rail depots, or hijack airships with Lyrandar crews? Is their motive hunger, revenge, or a payroll cut by a distant patron?

What prowls nearby; wolves, dire boar, or magebred spillovers? Do hunts keep numbers down, or do folk bar doors at dusk? Are rising attacks the result of Ashbound agitation, a failed druidic bargain, or habitat ruined by industry?
Is the town near Talenta migration routes or Q’barra jungles where dinosaurs roam, or do owlbears and other war beasts wander from old breeding pits? What wards, patrols, or payments hold the line?

entries.value: Environmental Hazards (Manifest Zones or Arcane Ruins)
entries.description: Does a Fernia-touched ravine set fields alight, or does a Mabar seep drain warmth and hope? Is the town perched near the Mournland where dead-grey mists swallow travellers? How have defences and daily routines adapted to survive?

Is sickness creeping along trade roads by water, beasts, or careless magic? Have Jorasco healers set up tents and tariffs; do quarantines hold? Is a cure brewing, or must someone fetch rare herbs from Lamannia-touched groves?

Do the dead rise after sunset, or does an aberrant taint twist minds and bodies? Is it tied to Khyber caverns, a Xoriat rift, or relics from a war lab? Can the source be sealed before the town changes beyond recognition?

entries.value: Economic Threat (House Pressure or Trade Wars)
entries.description: Has Orien rerouted the lightning rail, strangling commerce, or has a rival house undercut contracts? Are tariffs, embargoes, or Kundarak foreclosures looming? Who profits while the market withers, and what deals keep bread on tables?

Is this a border flare-up, Droaamite forays against Breland, or a warlord’s push to seize a pass? Are reinforcements coming, or must the town hire Deneith companies and hope for the best? Can parley save lives?
Do dolgaunts, mind flayers, or Inspired agents test the walls, or does Shavarath’s influence spill skirmishes into the fields? Why here; a weak spot, a prophecy mark, or a prize hidden below?
Does a nearby lord squeeze tithes and transit rights, or hide darker truth such as a vampire courtier or rakshasa in disguise? How do they shape local politics and trade; who dares expose them?

tables.title: Leisure
tables.description: Pastimes reveal values. In Eberron, recreation blends magic, martial skill, and house patronage; spectacle often hides schemes.

entries.value: Mount Racing (Hippogriffs, Clawfeet, or Magebred Beasts)
entries.description: Do hippogriffs wheel above or clawfeet thunder along a marked course with Vadalis breeding prizes? Is it a community diversion or a serious circuit that draws wagers from Orien factors and Phiarlan impresarios? Are courses warded with minor hazards to thrill the crowd?

Are archery meets, wrestling pits, and spell duels tied to Last War traditions or to militia training? Do winners earn medals, contracts, or invitations to a noble’s tourney? How safe are matches; are healing tents ready, and is cheating arcane or mundane?

entries.value: Ancient Sport or Modern Analog (Talenta Games)
entries.description: Are Talenta-inspired contests with trained dinosaurs presented respectfully, or has the town turned them into a Brelish-style league? What rules changed from the original rites; do culture-keepers object?

Do racers pilot soarsleds through floating rings while Lyrandar windwrights judge time, or ride Valenar-bred glidewings over cliff courses? Are tracks set with shifting platforms and minor illusions to test nerve?

Do local bards share the stage with House Phiarlan or Thuranni troupes? Are concerts enhanced by illusory scenery and harmonics that resonate with dragonshards? Which styles draw crowds; Aundairian ballads, Talenta rhythms, or Sharn’s experimental arcane sets?

entries.value: Games of Chance (Dragonshard Gambling or Arcane Houses)
entries.description: Are dice and cards augmented with enchanted decks and shard-powered wheels? Is there a discreet casino run by Boromar or a Tarkanan side room hidden behind a Ghallanda inn? Do Medani wards keep games fair, or do invisible hands tilt the odds?

entries.value: Board Games (Arcane Strategy or Dragonmarked Games)
entries.description: Do strategy boards echo Last War campaigns with animated pieces, or do houses sponsor branded variants whose tokens fight and fuse? Are champions minor celebrities with patrons and side bets?
Do sanctioned duels settle debts, or do underground pits run brutal matches that activists denounce—especially when warforged are exploited? Are spells limited, and who enforces the rules?
Is the town known for theatre backed by Phiarlan or Thuranni? Are shows elevated by illusions and living sets? Do scripts hide coded messages, and do tickets buy art, influence, or both?

Do daredevils race soarsleds and small elemental skiffs through sky gates while Orien and Lyrandar sponsor teams? Are courses dotted with harmless sparks and gusts; do rival houses sabotage rigs between heats?

tables.title: Main Industry
tables.description: Industry sets fortunes. Dragonshards, bound elementals, and trade routes attract houses, investors, and thieves in equal measure.

entries.description: Is the town built on a dragonshard field scouted by House Tharashk, or on veins of ore useful to Cannith for artifice? Who holds the charter; a house, a crown, or an Aurum-backed guild? Are miners free labourers or debt-bound crews; what hazards or mutations haunt the tunnels?

Diamonds fuel powerful spells. Who regulates the trade; a cautious crown, a Silver Flame prelacy, or Kundarak-secured syndicates? How tight is security against theft and sabotage; do Jorasco healers and Cannith enchanters cluster nearby to turn gems into services?

Do orchards use gentle elemental currents for climate and irrigation, or alchemical tonics for yield? Have wineries, syrupers, or potioners grown around the groves? Do Vadalis handlers and Jorasco herb-masters guide plant lines and health?

Do fleets brave Lamannia-touched shoals or Thelanis-odd tides along the Lhazaar? Are boats fitted with minor wards and spar-harpoons, or do captains trust seamanship alone? Which seasons bless or break the catch?

Do Vadalis herds and enchanted ploughs drive bumper harvests, or do traditionalists insist on old methods? How do goods move; Orien caravans, lightning rail sidings, or Lyrandar lifts? Which allied trades thrive; brewing, textiles, or alchemy?

entries.value: Trade Hub (Lightning Rail or Airship Port)
entries.description: Is the town a waystation with Orien sidings and Kundarak countinghouses, or a Lyrandar tower port? What cargos pass; staples, rare reagents, relics from Xen’drik? Is smuggling a nuisance or the second economy?

entries.value: Artisan Goods (Arcane Crafting or Cannith Forges)
entries.description: Do shops turn out enchanted armour, infused tools, and binding rings under Cannith supervision, or do independent guilds guard techniques? Are apprenticeships fair, or do secret oaths and non-competes trap talent?

What draws visitors; a manifest zone’s marvels, a battlefield memorial, or a revered shrine? Do festivals and Phiarlan shows fill inns, or does the town strain to host the crowds? Who manages the profits; Ghallanda, the temple, or the council?

Do yards assemble elemental galleons and skiffs for Lyrandar, or frames for airships with Cannith ringwork? Are clients naval, mercantile, or privateers? What risks loom; sabotage, stolen binders, or accidents with angry elementals?

Is there a college that trains magewrights, a Morgrave dig annex, or an Arcanix-affiliated lab? Do studies focus on planar theory, relics, or new artificer processes? How do scholars mix with miners, merchants, and priests in daily life?

tables.title: Events
tables.description: Festivals and gatherings mix pageantry with politics. Each celebration is a stage for deals, secrets, and danger.

entries.description: Is this a prestigious test tied to Aundairian martial culture, with imbued bows and moving illusory targets? Is it pure sport or bait to draw out a brigand? Can players enter, and what prizes—coin, contracts, or a named bow—await?

Jousts, duels, and melees bring nobles, Deneith champions, and house retainers. Must unknowns fake pedigree to compete, or do open lists exist? Which alliance, debt, or assassination plot hides behind the banners?

From blink-dog coursing in a Thelanis-touched dell to best harvest and craft-for-a-day challenges, do wards and illusions brighten the fair? Are rival families competing for bragging rights and contracts?

entries.value: Performance Festival (Carnival of Shadows or Phiarlan)
entries.description: Music, theatre, and acrobatics dazzle with illusion and shard harmonics. Is a Master Performer title at stake, or is the festival a cover for espionage with messages hidden in choreography?

Which patron or house foots the bill, and why; goodwill, distraction, or a signing ceremony? Do townsfolk accept the largesse or question motives? What deal is sealed behind the fireworks and free bread?
Do rites mark Siberys-fall omens or ward against Khyber’s depths? Are private ceremonies harmless remembrance, or Dragon Below liturgies that risk a breach? Are outsiders welcomed or watched?
Do arcane corn mazes and enchanted games enliven a traditional feast, or do elders keep it simple? What local customs bind the community, and who plans to turn revelry into riot or theft?

entries.value: Religious Holidays (Silver Flame, Sovereign Host, or Blood of Vol)
entries.description: Is the day solemn, celebratory, or both? Do processions and sermons unite the faithful, or do sects spar over doctrine? Do civic leaders use the moment for oaths and amnesties?

entries.value: Science and Magic Expo (Artifice and Arcane Inventions)
entries.description: Do Cannith artificers unveil new binders while magewrights show practical wonders? Are scholars from Morgrave, house buyers, and Aurum patrons in attendance? What malfunctions or sabotage spark chaos?

entries.value: Solstice Festivals (Planar Influence or Local Tradition)
entries.description: Do moon cycles and a Thelanis-touched grove shape rites, with gifts and bonfires, or do folk honour broader planar lore? Are strange sights—echoes, sprites, long shadows—considered blessings or warnings?

tables.title: Past
tables.description: What came before still speaks. Ruins, battlefields, and buried powers shape the present and tempt the bold.

entries.description: Was the town raised over a Dhakaani fort or vault? Do tunnels hold disciplined craftwork, war-song records, and locked armouries? How does this affect relations with Darguun envoys who claim ancestral rights?
Is the site part of lost giant works in Xen’drik or a transplanted structure studied by Morgrave teams? What magics linger; what traps still hum? Do treasure-hunters flood in when rumours spread?
Does Syrania buoy spirits and levitation, does Mabar dim lights and life, or does Thelanis paint the woods with story logic? How do people harness benefits while guarding against risks?
Was this a house-controlled quarry that birthed the town? Do shards still vein the earth and twist those who work too deep? Who holds old maps to unmined pockets below the streets?
Do Aereni stones and barrows mark undying guardianship, or do Valenar markers honour ancient battles? What relics remain; how do modern elves argue over heritage and rights?

entries.value: Dragon’s Resting Place (Ancient or Slumbering Dragon)
entries.description: Do legends tell of a dragon’s hoard beneath the town? Is the dragon gone, asleep, or watching through agents? Do runes, shard seams, or odd birthmarks hint at a deeper design; do the folk fear to stir it?
Do trenches, rusted engines, and cursed shell-holes ring the site? Are undead musters, haunted fogs, or dormant mines a constant threat? Who claims salvage rights and who mourns?
Do caverns pulse with old rites and organic stone; do oozes and aberrations nest within? Are secret societies still active, or did leaders bury the truth and hope it sleeps?
Do catacombs, murals, and lost wards underlie modern streets? What fall is remembered in half-truth, and which current feud echoes an age-old quarrel? Are scholars welcomed or run off?
Did Xoriat, Shavarath, or Dal Quor once press a foothold here? Do odd angles, lingering skirmishes, or dream-echoes persist? Is a breach due to reopen if signs align?

tables.title: Food
tables.description: Cuisine reflects land, magic, and trade. Magebreeding, manifest zones, and house logistics shape what fills bowls and cellars.

entries.description: Do rivers and coasts supply the staple catch, or do Vadalis pens raise hardier strains? Do manifest zones change flavour or properties? Which dishes and smokehouses define local taste, and what festivals bless the fleets?

Are cattle, sheep, and fowl raised in wide pastures, or do barns use minor elemental heating and Vadalis lines? Do markets prefer traditional breeds for ritual reasons, or newer stock for yield and resilience?
Do harsh lands favour clawfeet over cattle, with strict halfling-inspired husbandry and reverence? Are exotic meats rare delicacies rather than staples, with cultural taboos carefully observed?
Do druidic blessings or Thelanis-touched groves yield luminous greens and tonic fruits? Are plants valued for health, light, or stamina, and who controls seeds and cuttings?
Do fields grow wheat and oats under warded scarecrows and pest runes, or by hand and hoe? What breads, porridges, and festival pies travel well on Orien carts; what blights threaten the silo?
Are root cellars stocked with high-yield, hardy vegetables sustained by circles’ rites? Which stews, pickles, and spiced roasts mark seasons; what pests demand careful bargains rather than binding?
Do orchards bear fruit with minor restorative properties, sold fresh, preserved, or pressed? Are these luxuries for travellers or staples for locals; who holds grafting rights and rail contracts?
Do underfarms cultivate glowcaps and cavefish with gentle magic, providing steady fare for mining towns? Which recipes turn earthy harvests into comfort meals; how do traders supplement the diet?

Do beans and lentils rotate with grains to keep soil strong, or do simple enchantments boost yield? Are travel rations bean-based stews with regional spice, and do markets trade dried sacks by the cartload?

Eating sentient folk is an atrocity across the Five Nations. If it occurs, it is the act of depraved cults of the Dragon Below, monstrous tyrants, or starving war bands in the bleakest times. The Blood of Vol does not condone such practices. What factions fight to expose and end it; what scars linger if the truth comes out?

tables.title: Laws
tables.description: Local laws spring from hard lessons, hidden agendas, or superstition. Each restriction hints at a deeper story.

entries.description: Why ban performances; did a song trigger a magical disaster, or did Phiarlan theatre mask espionage? Do underground shows flourish with coded lyrics, and how has the silence reshaped the town’s mood?
Why clear the streets at night; Mabar-born shadows, cult meetings, or Dreaming Dark meddling? Are curfews enforced by wards and patrols, and what truly walks after dusk?

entries.value: Alcohol or Intoxicants (Prohibition or Magical Substances)
entries.description: Is alcohol restricted by moral code or public order concerns, and are narcotics such as dreamlily outlawed? Do Ghallanda inns face strict licensing; do Jorasco clinics treat addiction while Boromar or Daask run the black market?
How is a weapons ban enforced; surrender at the gate, Medani detection wards, or harsh penalties? Are Adventurers’ licences issued, and do secret duels settle grudges despite the law?
Which schools are restricted and why; Xoriat-touched horrors, political manipulation by enchantments, or prior summoning mishaps? Do black-market casters flourish, and who polices them?
Who is excluded and on what pretext; fear stoked by demagogues, wartime grudges, or a single crime made into policy? Do sympathisers shelter the banned, and what happens when the law is challenged?
Are certain histories, spells, or seditious pamphlets banned? Do Sivis-sealed notices and Zilargo-style ‘safety reviews’ mask censorship? How do scribes and students trade texts under watchful eyes?
Did an elemental engine failure or warforged riot lead to strict controls? Are artificers licensed and inspected, and are old engines locked in Kundarak vaults rather than repaired?
Has mockery been outlawed by a thin-skinned leader? Do secret plays and lampoons circulate anyway, and does humour slowly erode fear or draw arrests?

Is a faith barred due to past harm or present politics? Are all rites forbidden outside a state temple, or is a single sect targeted? Who enforces the ban—guards, zealots, or imported inquisitors—and where do hidden services meet?
